name: add_podman_mcp
description: Add/register a podman-isolated MCP server (e.g. taskmaster-mcp for high-level /task, godaddy-mcp, or any custom containerized connector) into the Super Grok environment using ONLY the desired effect: server name + podman image + optional envs. No need to manually edit toml, write launch scripts, or recall podman run flags. The TASK deduces the full stdio config using the real buildPodmanStdioConfig logic, updates ~/.grok/config.toml under [mcp_servers.<name>], and makes it immediately discoverable via search_tool + use_tool. Reusable for any podman MCP.

image.description: Full podman image ref, e.g. 'localhost/taskmaster-mcp:latest' or 'localhost/godaddy-mcp:latest'

env_vars.description: List of env var strings like 'GODADDY_API_KEY=xxx' or 'GITHUB_TOKEN=...'. Passed through to podman -e.

steps.description: Invoke real logic from claude-code/src/services/mcp/podman.ts : buildPodmanStdioConfig or buildPodmanStdioConfigFromAdd using the image + envs + extra args. Produces {command: 'podman', args: ['run', '-i', '--rm', ... , image]}
Persist the generated stdio server config under [mcp_servers.<name>] in ~/.grok/config.toml (local scope). Use direct file write or search_replace on the toml for the section with command/args/env.

Call search_tool with query matching the new name (e.g. 'taskmaster execute') to confirm the tools like taskmaster__list_available_tasks, taskmaster__save_task_definition etc. are now surfaced. Optionally run podman test or the /task sugar.
Optionally use linear or github to log the MCP registration as a completed task.
